commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=86ec4bd5be1699b617749c53274a82ed669a04dd)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a startup diagnostic for SwiftPM/ad-hoc dev builds, a local development note for CodexBarCache keychain prompts, and Swift Testing coverage for the pure hint builder.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path for the review finding: type-check the PR branch on macOS and the new Security symbols in `KeychainPromptCoordinator.swift` should require an explicit `import Security`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor action for real behavior proof, and the small code/doc blockers should be fixed before maintainers evaluate merge.

target.title: Help contributors diagnose keychain prompts from ad-hoc dev builds
target.body:
Summary:
- Add a local development note explaining why ad-hoc SwiftPM dev builds can trigger repeated CodexBarCache keychain prompts.
- Add a one-shot diagnostic warning when CodexBar detects a SwiftPM/ad-hoc dev build that may not match the keychain trusted-app ACL.
- Keep auth/keychain behavior unchanged: no env var, no bypass, no Keychain ACL changes.

Why:
Runtime evidence showed that running `.build/debug/CodexBar` can access keychain items with a signing identity that does not match `/Applications/CodexBar.app`. On macOS this can surface as repeated `ObjectAcl REJECTS`, `ACL partition mismatch`, and CodexBarCache prompts. The recommended dev workflows are to use `/Applications/CodexBar.app`, `Scripts/compile_and_run.sh`, or the existing Disable Keychain access development workaround.

Validation:
- `swift test --filter KeychainPromptCoordinatorTests`
- `swift test --filter "KeychainPromptCoordinator|KeychainCacheStore|KeychainNoUIQuery|KeychainAccessGate|KeychainPromptSafetyAudit"`

Notes:
- This replaces the earlier Claude-only env-var bypass direction with a safer contributor-facing diagnostic.
- This does not change production auth behavior.
- This does not bypass Keychain reads.
- This does not modify Keychain ACL construction.
target.author: yuxin-qiao

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
